Day One · Strategy synthesis

Export sales needs an operating system.

A working thesis for an evidence-backed, agent-run pipeline built for Chinese manufacturers entering overseas markets.

Working thesisFounder alignment deck · 18 Jul 2026
01 / 16
Chinese manufacturing × agent systemsInternal strategy · not traction
The Day One decision

One core. One wedge. One company to test.

Commercial wedge

Help export-ready Chinese manufacturers create qualified overseas opportunities.

Start at market choice, account research and buyer relevance—not mass sending.

Technical core

Choose the next best workflow under quality, cost, latency and permission constraints.

The core is a planner plus source-backed knowledge, safe execution and outcome evaluation.

Go-to-market

Sell a managed operating service first. Productize repeated decisions later.

User: export BD. Economic buyer: CEO or head of international sales.

Not proven yet: lower CAC than trade shows, end-to-end conversion, autonomous planning, or a repeatable manufacturing ICP.
02 / 16
Confirmed · Hypothesis · Next proofDay One synthesis
Problem definition

Market entry is a learning problem disguised as lead generation.

Manufacturers already have channels. What they lack is a loop that turns each market signal, decision and outcome into a better next move.

Today: rented access, fragmented memory

1Trade shows, marketplaces and brokers produce episodic lead bursts.
2Data, CRM, copy and channel tools each own a narrow activity.
3Senior BD people carry relevance, language and product context in their heads.
4Failure is blamed on “the message,” not traced to market, buyer, proof, timing or channel.
×

Opportunity: own the decision loop

1Choose a market and ICP from explicit product and delivery constraints.
2Ground every account thesis in current, reviewable evidence.
3Decide research more, act, hand off—or stop.
4Write replies, RFQs and losses back into company-owned knowledge.
Compounding value: the next market costs less to understand because the system remembers what was true, what failed and why.
03 / 16
Core insightOptimize decisions, not activity
Stakeholder system

Sales, CEOs and analytics are not three segments.

They are three contracts inside one buying system. The overseas buyer—who never pays us—still bears the brand and relevance risk.

Export BD
Daily user
Give me fewer, better accounts—and a credible next action.
Research time · accepted briefs · edit rate · qualified conversations
CEO / Head of Intl.
Economic buyer
Build owned pipeline without adding overseas SDR headcount too early.
Cost per qualified opportunity · pipeline visibility · brand risk · data ownership
Analytics / RevOps
Evidence owner
Show which market, evidence and action produced a real commercial outcome.
Trace completeness · funnel definitions · total cost · causal learning
Overseas buyer
Recipient
Contact me only when the supplier has relevant proof and a low-friction ask.
Wrong-person rate · proof quality · response quality · respectful opt-out

Gatekeepers: product engineering, marketing, legal/compliance, CRM owner and the quote owner. Improving one contract cannot come at the expense of another.

04 / 16
User ≠ buyer ≠ evidence ownerOne operating system
ICP recommendation · Hypothesis

Start with export-ready, spec-driven manufacturers—not “all Chinese factories.”

Candidate segmentPublic evidenceValue per winWorkflow fitDecision
Industrial components / OEM partsHigh: specs, certifications, buyer use casesHigh, often repeatableStrong: research changes relevanceBeachhead
Light machinery / automationHigh: technical proof and applicationsVery highStrong, but quote and service complexity riseNext
Private-label consumer goodsMedium; differentiation is often thinMediumWeak: volume and price dominateAvoid first
Regulated medical / chemicalHigh, but compliance-heavyVery highRequires deep domain and legal expertiseDefer
Pilot filter

Export-ready, not export-curious

  • One named country or region
  • English product proof and certifications
  • A senior owner for replies and quotes
  • Order economics can absorb a managed pilot
  • A recent trigger: launch, capacity, certification, weak channel or new-market mandate

Negative ICP: price-only catalog, no proof, no reply owner, or a motion that depends on unofficial mass automation.

05 / 16
Recommendation, not market evidenceValidate through discovery
Value proposition

Sell the first evidence loop—not the whole export department.

North-star promise

An owned, always-on overseas sales pipeline.

Turn product knowledge into qualified, contract-ready opportunities with measurable cost per opportunity.

This is the vision. “Self-running,” “converts customers” and “a fraction of trade-show cost” require customer benchmarks before they become sales claims.
First sellable offer

Ten evidence-backed accounts in 14 days.

For one market: buyer maps, why-now evidence, send / research-more / do-not-send decisions, localized drafts, human approval and a reviewable CRM handoff.

What the customer buys: better market-entry decisions and an evidence baseline—not a guaranteed meeting count or autonomous LinkedIn.
06 / 16
Vision ≠ first contractOutcome before automation
Export value chain

The wedge sits between product knowledge and a qualified opportunity.

Quote, negotiation, contract, CAD changes and customer lifecycle matter—but they are expansion, not Day One scope.

Initial product boundary

Factory truth

Specs, proof, MOQ, capacity

Market choice

Country, segment, trigger

Account evidence

Fit, signals, exclusions

Buyer map

Role, need, why now

Allowed action

Draft, approve, execute

Qualified opportunity

Problem, fit, next step

Quote & negotiate

Price, terms, materials

Contract & adapt

Legal, CAD, production

Deliver & retain

Service, reorder, referrals

Own the upstream evidence ledger now; earn the right to expand downstream later.

07 / 16
Current core: market → qualified opportunityExpansion shown in low contrast
Use cases become one loop

The product is a controlled loop: research → decide → act → learn.

01

Define the job

One market, product, buyer type, trigger, offer, proof and exclusion set.

02

Source evidence

Public signals, customer documents, CRM history and product constraints—with provenance.

03

Build the account thesis

Who may buy, why now, which proof matters and what is still uncertain.

04

Choose or abstain

Research more, human review, allowed action, handoff—or do not send.

05

Execute within permission

Email where allowed; LinkedIn as a human task; WhatsApp only with auditable opt-in.

06

Qualify and hand off

Pause on reply, classify, escalate ambiguity and route RFQ / quote context to sales.

07

Write back truth

CRM owns account state, consent, outcome and next action. Memory can suggest, never overwrite.

08

Improve the next plan

Use human edits, rejection reasons, cost, latency and outcomes to change the playbook.

08 / 16
Permission-aware by designCRM remains customer-owned truth
Technology / science

The planner chooses the minimum sufficient next step.

Commercial task contract

Target outcome, quality threshold, budget, deadline, risk tier and human owner.

Constrained workflow planner

Choose research depth, retrieval, model, tool, human escalation, action or stop.

Knowledge & context system

Raw evidence → canonical CRM facts → cited context, with freshness and conflict handling.

Safe action runtime

Exact approval, policy, suppression, idempotency, rate limit, audit and kill switch.

Outcome & evaluation ledger

Plan trace, sources, tokens, tool cost, latency, human edits, replies, RFQs and outcomes.

Built today

Auditable control plane

78/78 local tests cover core safety primitives and channel boundaries.

Technical thesis

Adaptive planner + retrieval

No current LLM planner, model router, token accounting or production knowledge retriever.

Proof required

Baseline and ablations

Compare fixed workflow, full context and strong-model-only against adaptive planning in shadow mode.

09 / 16
Science direction ≠ shipped capabilityEvidence boundary preserved
Joint optimization

We optimize inside a safety envelope—not with a vanity score.

Optimization target
Maximize correct commercial decisions and outcome value.
Hard gate 1: unauthorized, duplicate or suppressed action = 0
Hard gate 2: decision quality stays above the agreed threshold
Budget: token, tool, data and review cost stay within bounds
Latency: the workflow meets p95 time-to-decision targets
Cheap and fast cannot compensate for a wrong buyer, unsupported claim or policy violation.

Quality

ICP precision · claim grounding · reviewer agreement · qualified opportunity rate

Fewer wrong accounts, less brand risk, better conversations

Cost

Tokens + data + tools + human review per approved account / outcome

Lower research and delivery cost—not token savings in isolation

Latency

Trigger-to-approved action · reply-to-pause · time-to-account-brief

Act while the market signal is still useful

Memory

Citation coverage · stale/conflict errors · reuse without repeated research

Market knowledge survives employee and vendor changes

Joint proof

Share of runs meeting quality, safety, cost and latency SLOs together

Show a Pareto improvement against a fixed workflow baseline

10 / 16
Optimize the cost and speed of a correct decisionHard constraints first
Competition map · Public positioning

Direct competitors automate acquisition. The open wedge is deal execution.

Execution autonomy Sales lifecycle depth Exposure / listsContract-ready opportunity Plan + act + learn Canton Fair
Alibaba.com Sales Navigator
Apollo Artisan · 11x
iSales OKKI
AiReach + CRM Accio Work NOLVO / local
sales agency Target position

Closest direct competitor

OKKI already combines foreign-trade data, multichannel reach, CRM, opportunity support and document understanding.

Largest strategic threat

Accio Work combines agent capability with Alibaba distribution and data. Platform independence must be real.

Defensible wedge

Manufacturer product knowledge, post-reply execution, permission-aware actions and an outcome-linked learning loop.

Positions reflect official public product claims as of 18 Jul 2026—not measured performance. See source appendix.

11 / 16
No “zero competition” claimCompete on decision depth
Value chain strategy

Own the decision layer. Rent the commodity infrastructure.

The company should accumulate judgment and outcomes—not rebuild every model, connector or customer system.

Own

Decision intelligence

Identity, evidence, manufacturer knowledge model, planner, policy, evaluation and the outcome-linked playbook.

Buy

Replaceable infrastructure

LLMs, cloud, search, data providers and official channel transport—behind vendor-neutral contracts and adapters.

Customer-owned

Commercial truth and assets

Domain, mailbox, CRM, WABA, LinkedIn seat, product documents, customer history, consent and suppression.

Integrate later

Downstream execution

Quote, contract, CAD, ERP, production and customer success—only after one upstream workflow repeats.

12 / 16
Client truth stays portableVendor-neutral by design
Potential defensibility

The moat begins only when outcomes train the next decision.

Outcome-linked
decision data
Verified product + market evidence
Better account thesis and plan
Replies, RFQs, quotes and losses
Human corrections + policy
↘↘↘↘

What can compound

  • Manufacturer-specific models of specs, certifications, buyers, objections and market rules
  • Planner calibration: what to research, how deeply, which route to use and when to stop
  • Permission-aware execution embedded in real CRM and approval workflows
  • Decision traces linked to qualified opportunities—not synthetic scores
Not a moat yet: prompts, base models, ordinary vector search, connectors, synthetic data or an unbenchmarked “planning algorithm.”
13 / 16
Potential flywheel · not current proofCustomer data boundaries matter
30-day evidence plan

Prove one narrow market before building more automation.

Week 01

Find the historical pain

  • 3–6 CEO / export-BD interviews
  • One real failed campaign, show or platform episode each
  • Baseline time, spend, leads and outcomes
Gate: repeated problem + named owner
Week 02

Define the decision contract

  • One country, buyer type and trigger
  • 20 candidate accounts
  • Product proof, exclusions and channel rules
Gate: 10 accounts can share one thesis
Week 03

Run the manual baseline

  • 10 evidence-backed account briefs
  • Client review and send / no-send
  • Human action only after policy gate
Gate: customer spends time correcting the work
Week 04

Review outcome and economics

  • Replies, qualified conversations or RFQs
  • Tokens, tools and founder review minutes
  • Stop / revise / paid pilot decision
Gate: E3/E4 commitment, not demo praise

Advance when: a manufacturer provides real product / CRM artifacts, names an owner, agrees on success criteria and commits to the next decision.

Kill or rewrite when: ten accounts lack credible why-now evidence, every customer needs a different workflow, or margin depends on hidden founder labor.

14 / 16
Evidence ladder: history → artifact → pilot → paymentNo volume vanity
Founder alignment

What is true. What is assumed. What must be proved next.

Confirmed

We know the shape of the system.

  • BD is the user; CEO / head of international is the payer
  • The workflow spans research, permission, action, reply and CRM learning
  • A tested safety control plane exists locally
  • Direct Chinese foreign-trade AI competitors already exist
Assumed

The manufacturing wedge is attractive.

  • Spec-driven, export-ready firms feel this pain acutely
  • Better account decisions can beat channel-dependent acquisition economics
  • A planner can improve quality, cost and latency together
  • Post-reply execution creates a defendable gap
Next proof

One narrow evidence loop.

  • One product category and overseas market
  • Three concrete historical pain events
  • Ten researched accounts and client corrections
  • A paid or artifact-backed pilot commitment
  • A baseline the planner can eventually beat

The next milestone is not more automation. It is one real market with one complete evidence loop.

15 / 16
Decision: validate the wedgeThen earn autonomy
Appendix · Sources

Sources, evidence boundary and method.

Primary workshop evidence

Four Day One workshop photographs supplied by the founders, 18 Jul 2026.

Local product and technical audit

ai-agent-outbound/README.md, GOAL.md, Founder Thesis, Client Service Assembly and Validation Ledger.

Technical interpretation

Current code proves a permission-aware control plane. Planner, retrieval integration, token accounting, model routing and joint optimization are labelled as thesis / roadmap.

Method

Whiteboard statements were separated into Confirmed, Hypothesis and Next proof. No TAM, traction, ROI or customer outcome was invented.
